nodes.context: A subset of the Bloods gang alliance, founded in 1969 in Compton, California. Involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder.
A larger gang alliance formed in the early 1970s in Los Angeles, primarily as a rival to the Crips. Includes the Pirus and other sets.
A major street gang founded in Los Angeles in the late 1960s by Raymond Washington and Stanley Williams. Known for wearing blue and rivalry with Bloods.
A smaller street gang that allied with the Piru Street Boys to form the Bloods alliance.
A smaller street gang that allied with the Piru Street Boys to form the Bloods alliance.
A smaller street gang that allied with the Piru Street Boys to form the Bloods alliance.
A Piru set that developed a rivalry with Fruit Town Piru in the late 1990s, resulting in murders.
A Piru set that developed a rivalry with Mob Piru in the late 1990s.
A subculture and gang associated with the Insane Clown Posse, listed as allies of the Pirus in the infobox.

edges.context: The Pirus are a subset of the larger Bloods gang alliance.
The Pirus formed as a rival to the Crips, leading to violent gang wars.
The Bloods and Crips are long-standing rivals, with violent conflicts over territory and influence.
The Piru Street Boys were initially associated with the Crips but later had a falling-out, though some sources dispute any alliance.
The Piru Street Boys formed an alliance with the Brims to oppose the Crips, leading to the formation of the Bloods.
The Piru Street Boys formed an alliance with the Bishops to oppose the Crips.
The Piru Street Boys formed an alliance with the Denver Lanes to oppose the Crips.
Mob Piru and Fruit Town Piru developed a rivalry by the late 1990s, resulting in murders.
The infobox lists Juggalos as allies of the Pirus.
